body_txt: There is just something about bladed weapons that naturally draws people to them. Maybe it's the brilliant shine a well-polished blade gives or the hundreds upon hundreds of intricately designed swords, knives, and other blades that exist around the world. This is not weird, though. Since the dawn of humanity, knives have been one of the earliest tools we have used as a means of survival. Thus, knives have influenced human civilization for tens of thousands of years in many cultures throughout the world, where they are often depicted within religious or ceremonial contexts. Whatever the reason, our admiration towards bladed weapons has made its influence through pop culture. Enter the character who has a keen interest in blades. They can tell the difference between every weapon in their collection, no matter how small. They love knives so much that they can't help but gush about them to anyone who will hear. Many blade enthusiasts love the danger and morbidity associated with knives so quite a few of them double as Nightmare Fetishists or Psycho Knife Nuts , while others, especially those with animosity for ranged weapons, consider Firearms Are Cowardly or Guns Are Worthless . Some collectors only collect knives and other blades for show and never intend to use them as weapons, while others carry hundreds of knives on their person at all times. The blade enthusiast's collection might be mounted on a Wall of Weapons . Subtrope of Otaku . Related to Heroes Prefer Swords , in which both can overlap for justification. See also Gun Nut , which is for gun enthusiasts. For characters who primarily use knives or other bladed weapons that reflect their personality, see Weapon-Based Characterization . For characters who collect unorthodox items, see Collector of the Strange .
